div.p: Brother Joseph then addressed the Twelve, and said that in all our counsels, especially while on trial of any one, we should see and observe all things appertaining to the subject, and discern the spirit1 by which either party was governed. We should be in a situation to understand every spirit and judge righteous judgment2 and not be asleep.3 We should keep order and not let the council be imposed upon by unruly conduct.4 The Saints need not think because I am familiar with them and am playful and cheerful, that I am ignorant of what is going on.5 Iniquity of any kind cannot be sustained in the Church,6 and it will not fare well where I am; for I am determined while I do lead the Church, to lead it right.7 (May 27, 1843.) DHC 5:411.
div.div: Testimony Concerning Brigham Young and H. C. Kimball
div.p: Of the Twelve Apostles chosen in Kirtland, and ordained under the hands of Oliver Cowdery, David Whitmer and myself,8 there have been but two9 but what have lifted their heel against me10—namely Brigham Young and Heber C. Kimball. (May 28, 1843.) DHC 5:412.
div.div: The Purpose of the Gathering of Israel
div.p: A large assembly of the Saints met at the Temple stand. Hymn by the choir. Prayer by Elder Parley P. Pratt, and singing.

President Joseph Smith remarked—“I am a rough stone. The sound of the hammer and chisel was never heard on me until the Lord took me in hand. I desire the learning and wisdom of heaven alone.11 I have not the least idea, if Christ should come to the earth and preach such rough things as He preached to the Jews, but that this generation would reject Him12 for being so rough.”

He then took for his text the 37th verse of 23rd chapter of Matthew—“O Jerusalem, Jerusalem, thou that killest the prophets and stonest them which are sent unto thee; how often would I have gathered thy children together, even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, and ye would not.”13
This subject was presented to me since I came to the stand. What was the object of gathering the Jews, or the people of
